title: Person killed in Mattapan shooting, 2 others shot in Dorchester overnight Saturday
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: One man was killed in a shooting Saturday night on Woolson Street in Mattapan, according to Boston police.
Two people were injured in a separate shooting early Sunday morning near Geneva Avenue in Dorchester.
Investigations are ongoing for both shootings, with no arrests reported in the Dorchester incident.

title: Kevin Garnett wasn’t a fan of Jayson Tatum missing Game 7 vs. Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Garnett was shocked Jayson Tatum missed Game 7 against the Sixers, citing morale issues for the Celtics.
Tatum was ruled out due to left knee stiffness, with the injury report updated two hours before the game.
Paul Pierce supported the decision to sit Tatum, emphasizing long-term health over risking further injury.

title: 2 people wounded, 1 seriously, after shooting in Dorchester
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A shooting on Kingsdale Street in Dorchester left a woman with life-threatening injuries and a man with non-life-threatening injuries.
Boston police are seeking witnesses and video evidence for the incident, which they believe was a targeted attack.
The shooting scene remains under investigation with no arrests made as of Sunday night, according to police.

title: NBA insider: Atlanta Hawks were interested in trade for Jaylen Brown last year
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Atlanta Hawks expressed interest in trading for Jaylen Brown, a Georgia native, last summer.
Discussion between the Hawks and Celtics stalled due to Brown's large contract, the richest in NBA history.
NBA insider suggests the Hawks could revisit trading for Brown, amid reports of his frustration with the Celtics.

title: Woman charged in Southborough fatal crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Abigail Throckmorton, 23, charged with motor vehicle homicide after fatal crash in Southborough in February.
Throckmorton allegedly drove on wrong side of Route 9 at 70 mph, resulting in head-on collision.
Victim Crystal Johnson-Clementi, 37, died in the crash; Throckmorton set for pretrial hearing on June 30.

title: What’s causing an engine shake in my 10-year-old SUV?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2016 Audi Q5 owner reports engine shake at highway speeds with check engine and EPC lights on.
AAA's Car Doctor advises towing the vehicle to a mechanic due to possible engine misfire and throttle system issues.
A flashing check engine light indicates a serious misfire, and driving could cause further damage, necessitating professional assessment.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox fall flat against Rays on Mother’s Day
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Boston Red Sox fell to the Tampa Bay Rays 4-1 on Mother's Day, struggling offensively at Fenway Park.
Starter Payton Tolle allowed three earned runs over five innings; error by Trevor Story impacted the game's outcome.
Willson Contreras left the game with a right-hand contusion; initial scans were negative, but he's day-to-day.

title: Boston gang members, associates facing slew of firearm, drug trafficking charges
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Eight members and associates of the Columbia Point Dawgs and Johnston Road gangs face firearm and drug trafficking charges.
Federal investigation seized 18 firearms, over 20 machine gun conversion devices, and large quantities of fentanyl and crack cocaine.
Defendants include residents from Massachusetts and Florida, with charges ranging from firearm dealing to fentanyl distribution.

title: Photos: Scenes from a fun, but soggy, Saturday in Greater Boston
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Families participated in LEGO Group's Duckling Day on Boston Common, celebrating 'Make Way for Ducklings' despite rain.
Somerville's PorchFest featured live music performances, attracting crowds to porches across the city.
Mojo Boston Music Festival held in City Hall Plaza offered a 10-hour event with contemporary sound and urban art.

title: New Bedford uncle pleads guilty to killing nephew during family altercation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Juan Padilla-Santana, 43, pleaded guilty to manslaughter for killing his nephew, Omil Padilla-Corsino, during a family altercation.
Padilla-Santana was sentenced to 12 ½ to 17 ½ years in prison by Fall River Superior Court on Wednesday.
The stabbing occurred on August 24, 2024, in New Bedford, following an argument involving family members.
